---
author:
 - Christian Gutschow
 - Christine Stawitz
 - David Pérez Suárez
 - Duncan Leggat
 - Jeremy Stein
 - Jyoti Bhogal
 - Patrick J. Roddy
 - Samuel Avis
date: 2026-04-30
format: revealjs
institute:
 - Advanced Research Computing, UCL
 - Advanced Research Computing, UoB
 - Advanced Research Computing, UCL
 - Advanced Research Computing, UCL
 - Advanced Research Computing, UCL
 - RSE Asia Association
 - Advanced Research Computing, UCL
 - ICCS, University of Cambridge
subtitle: Collaborations Workshop 2026
title: CI Usage Badge
---

# What's CI Usage?

https://youtu.be/kscfDFtImbM?si=VmI6N0Yog73ckmsY&t=7
https://youtu.be/kscfDFtImbM?si=VmI6N0Yog73ckmsY&t=20


## The problem

People use the big shared computer too much.

Some people use it more than others and this is not good to the other people, because then they cannot use the big shared computer when they need it.


## Solution

We have told a smaller computer to check how much each person is using the big shared computer and draw a picture to make it extra clear.

This will help people know that they are using the big shared computer too much.

## What's available

Some have done something similar: [green-ci](https://cambridge-iccs.github.io/green-ci/)

# What we've used?

We talk with the computers with snakes, pirates, mermaids, but no robots!

# Demo

<!-- embed website -->

<!-- add asset of bubbles graph in assets -->

# Who did what?

- Duncan: master of snakes
- Paddy: master of cat with many legs
- Christine and Jyoti: Our favourite pirates
- Sam: friend of the mermaids
- Chris: drinks the energy
- Jeremy: chief writer
- David: social butterfly

# Future

<!-- include diagram from : https://github.com/green-actions/green-managing/issues/8#issuecomment-4352890945 -->
